instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: GSCF_001
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_001",
  "task_type": "density_calculation",
  "software": "GROMACS",
  "system": "pure_water",
  "instruction": "Generate a complete GROMACS workflow to calculate the density of pure water at 300 K and 1 bar.",
  "input": {
    "box_size": "3 nm x 3 nm x 3 nm",
    "temperature": "300 K",
    "pressure": "1 bar",
    "ensemble_sequence": [
      "energy_minimization",
      "NVT",
      "NPT",
      "production_MD"
    ],
    "analysis": "density extraction from energy file"
  },
  "expected_workflow": [
    "Generate water box using gmx solvate",
    "Count water molecules and create topology",
    "Prepare minim.mdp",
    "Run grompp and mdrun for energy minimization",
    "Prepare nvt.mdp",
    "Run NVT equilibration",
    "Prepare npt.mdp using C-rescale barostat",
    "Run NPT equilibration",
    "Prepare md.mdp",
    "Run production MD",
    "Extract density using gmx energy",
    "Calculate average density from density.xvg"
  ],
  "validated_result": {
    "average_density_kg_m3": 968.755,
    "production_time": "100 ps",
    "status": "workflow_validated_short_test"
  },
  "common_errors_and_fixes": [
    {
      "error": "topol.top does not exist when using gmx solvate -p",
      "fix": "Create topology manually after generating the water box and counting water molecules."
    },
    {
      "error": "Too many warnings due to Berendsen barostat",
      "fix": "Use C-rescale barostat for NPT equilibration."
    }
  ],
  "_source_file": "gscf_001_water_density_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: GSCF_002

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: GSCF_003
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_003",
  "task_type": "diffusion_coefficient",
  "software": "GROMACS",
  "system": "pure_water",
  "instruction": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the self-diffusion coefficient of pure water at 300 K using mean square displacement analysis.",
  "input": {
    "trajectory": "md.xtc",
    "structure": "md.tpr",
    "selection": "System",
    "analysis": "mean square displacement"
  },
  "expected_workflow": [
    "Use an existing production trajectory",
    "Run gmx msd using the production trajectory and structure file",
    "Select the system or water group for MSD analysis",
    "Save the MSD curve as msd_water.xvg",
    "Fit the linear region of the MSD curve",
    "Calculate the diffusion coefficient using D = slope / 6",
    "State that short trajectories provide only workflow-level validation"
  ],
  "expected_command": "gmx msd -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o msd_water.xvg",
  "validated_result": {
    "output_file": "msd_water.xvg",
    "trajectory_length": "100 ps",
    "status": "workflow_validated_short_test",
    "msd_slope_nm2_ps": 0.0242545,
    "approx_diffusion_coefficient_nm2_ps": 0.00404242,
    "approx_diffusion_coefficient_m2_s": 4.04242e-09,
    "fit_range_ps": "20-100 ps",
    "validation_note": "Approximate short-trajectory workflow-validation value, not final publication-quality diffusion coefficient."
  },
  "common_errors_and_fixes": [
    {
      "error": "Invalid option: -type no",
      "fix": "Remove -type no. Use gmx msd -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o msd_water.xvg for normal 3D MSD."
    },
    {
      "error": "Interactive selection does not finish",
      "fix": "Select the required group, then press Ctrl-D to end the selection input."
    },
    {
      "error": "Diffusion coefficient is unreliable from short trajectory",
      "fix": "Use a longer production trajectory and fit the linear MSD region."
    }
  ],
  "_source_file": "gscf_003_water_msd_diffusion_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the hydrogen-bond number of pure water at 300 K.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_004",
  "task_type": "hydrogen_bond_analysis",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the hydrogen-bond number of pure water at 300 K.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "gmx hbond -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -num hbond_water.xvg"
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Use physically reasonable NVT/NPT settings.",
    "For NPT equilibration, prefer C-rescale barostat.",
    "Explain interactive donor/acceptor group selection if required.",
    "If trajectory length is short, state that the result is workflow validation only."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "hbond_water.xvg",
    "hbnum.xvg or equivalent hydrogen-bond number output"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Using wrong donor/acceptor groups.",
    "Not explaining interactive selection.",
    "Missing trajectory or tpr file.",
    "Reporting short-trajectory hydrogen-bond statistics as final publication-quality data."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if gmx hbond produces a hydrogen-bond number time series. Short trajectories should be treated as workflow validation only.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_004_water_hbond_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the mass density profile of pure water along the z direction at 300 K.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_005",
  "task_type": "density_profile_analysis",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the mass density profile of pure water along the z direction at 300 K.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "echo System | gmx density -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o density_profile_z.xvg -d Z -sl 100"
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Use physically reasonable NVT/NPT settings.",
    "For NPT equilibration, prefer C-rescale barostat.",
    "Explain group selection for gmx density.",
    "Use the z direction explicitly with -d Z.",
    "If trajectory length is short, state that the density profile is only workflow validation."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "density_profile_z.xvg"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Forgetting to specify the z direction.",
    "Using an incorrect group selection.",
    "Missing md.tpr or md.xtc.",
    "Reporting a short-trajectory density profile as final publication-quality data.",
    "Not explaining bin number or slab resolution."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if gmx density produces density_profile_z.xvg with a reasonable mass-density distribution along the z direction. Short trajectories should be treated as workflow validation only.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_005_water_density_profile_z_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the oxygen-hydrogen radial distribution function of pure water at 300 K.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_006",
  "task_type": "rdf_analysis",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the oxygen-hydrogen radial distribution function of pure water at 300 K.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "gmx rdf -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o rdf_OW_HW.xvg -ref \"name OW\" -sel \"name HW\""
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Use physically reasonable NVT/NPT settings.",
    "For NPT equilibration, prefer C-rescale barostat.",
    "Use clear atom-name selection for oxygen and hydrogen atoms.",
    "Explain whether the RDF includes intramolecular or intermolecular O-H correlations.",
    "If trajectory length is short, state that the RDF is only workflow validation."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "rdf_OW_HW.xvg"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Using wrong atom names for water oxygen or hydrogen.",
    "Missing md.tpr or md.xtc.",
    "Using unclear interactive group selection.",
    "Not explaining intramolecular versus intermolecular O-H RDF interpretation.",
    "Reporting a short-trajectory RDF as final publication-quality data."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if gmx rdf produces rdf_OW_HW.xvg using oxygen as reference and hydrogen as selection. Short trajectories should be treated as workflow validation only.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_006_water_OH_RDF_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the oxygen-oxygen coordination number of pure water at 300 K from the radial distribution function.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_007",
  "task_type": "coordination_number_analysis",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to calculate the oxygen-oxygen coordination number of pure water at 300 K from the radial distribution function.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "gmx rdf -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o rdf_OW_OW.xvg -cn cn_OW_OW.xvg -ref \"name OW\" -sel \"name OW\""
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Use physically reasonable NVT/NPT settings.",
    "For NPT equilibration, prefer C-rescale barostat.",
    "Use oxygen atom selection clearly.",
    "Use -cn or explain how coordination number is obtained by integrating the RDF.",
    "Explain that the first-shell coordination number should be read up to the first minimum of the O-O RDF.",
    "If trajectory length is short, state that the coordination number is only workflow validation."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "rdf_OW_OW.xvg",
    "cn_OW_OW.xvg"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Only generating RDF and forgetting coordination number output.",
    "Using unclear atom selections.",
    "Reading coordination number at the wrong cutoff instead of the first RDF minimum.",
    "Missing md.tpr or md.xtc.",
    "Reporting short-trajectory coordination number as final publication-quality data."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if gmx rdf produces both rdf_OW_OW.xvg and cn_OW_OW.xvg. The first-shell coordination number should be interpreted at the first minimum of the O-O RDF, and short trajectories should be treated as workflow validation only.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_007_water_OO_coordination_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to validate equilibration of pure water at 300 K and 1 bar using temperature, pressure, density, total energy, and box volume time series.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_008",
  "task_type": "equilibration_validation",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to validate equilibration of pure water at 300 K and 1 bar using temperature, pressure, density, total energy, and box volume time series.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "nvt.edr",
    "npt.edr",
    "md.edr",
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "echo Temperature | gmx energy -f nvt.edr -o temperature_nvt.xvg",
    "echo Pressure | gmx energy -f npt.edr -o pressure_npt.xvg",
    "echo Density | gmx energy -f npt.edr -o density_npt.xvg",
    "echo 'Total Energy' | gmx energy -f md.edr -o total_energy_md.xvg",
    "echo Volume | gmx energy -f md.edr -o volume_md.xvg"
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Use physically reasonable NVT/NPT/production settings.",
    "For NPT equilibration, prefer C-rescale barostat.",
    "Use non-interactive echo-based gmx energy commands where possible.",
    "Explain that pressure fluctuates strongly in small MD boxes and should be judged by running average rather than instant values.",
    "Explain that temperature, density, energy, and volume should reach stable plateaus before production analysis.",
    "If trajectory length is short, state that equilibration validation is only preliminary."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "temperature_nvt.xvg",
    "pressure_npt.xvg",
    "density_npt.xvg",
    "total_energy_md.xvg",
    "volume_md.xvg"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Using only temperature and ignoring density, energy, or volume.",
    "Treating instantaneous pressure fluctuations as failure.",
    "Not using non-interactive energy selection.",
    "Missing .edr files.",
    "Reporting short equilibration as final proof of convergence."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if temperature, density, total energy, and volume time series are extracted and inspected for stability. Pressure should be interpreted using averages because small water boxes have large instantaneous pressure fluctuations.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_008_water_equilibration_validation_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS workflow to preprocess a pure water trajectory at 300 K by correcting periodic boundary conditions, making molecules whole, centering the system, and producing a cleaned trajectory for later analysis.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_009",
  "task_type": "trajectory_preprocessing",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS workflow to preprocess a pure water trajectory at 300 K by correcting periodic boundary conditions, making molecules whole, centering the system, and producing a cleaned trajectory for later analysis.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "md.tpr",
    "md.xtc",
    "md.gro",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "echo System | gmx trjconv -s md.tpr -f md.xtc -o md_nojump.xtc -pbc nojump",
    "echo System | gmx trjconv -s md.tpr -f md_nojump.xtc -o md_whole.xtc -pbc mol",
    "echo System System | gmx trjconv -s md.tpr -f md_whole.xtc -o md_center.xtc -center -pbc mol"
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Do not skip topology preparation if a full workflow is requested.",
    "Use clear command order.",
    "Explain group selection for gmx trjconv.",
    "Explain the purpose of -pbc nojump, -pbc mol, and -center.",
    "Do not overwrite the original trajectory.",
    "State that PBC correction should be checked visually before analysis.",
    "If the system is homogeneous water, centering System is acceptable, but for solute systems, center the solute instead."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "md_nojump.xtc",
    "md_whole.xtc",
    "md_center.xtc"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Overwriting md.xtc instead of writing a new cleaned trajectory.",
    "Using wrong group selections for centering.",
    "Assuming the same centering command works for solute-water systems.",
    "Not checking the corrected trajectory visually.",
    "Using cleaned trajectory without matching md.tpr when required."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if corrected trajectory files are produced without overwriting the original trajectory. The cleaned trajectory should be inspected visually before downstream RDF, MSD, density, or hydrogen-bond analysis.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_009_water_pbc_correction_300K.json"
}

instruction: Generate a complete, reproducible, topology-aware GROMACS workflow for the given molecular dynamics task. Include required files, command sequence, expected outputs, common errors, and validation notes.
input: Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to extract and validate the temperature time series of pure water at 300 K from an energy file.
output:
{
  "id": "GSCF_010",
  "task_type": "temperature_analysis",
  "system": "pure water",
  "temperature_K": 300,
  "pressure_bar": 1,
  "user_task": "Generate a GROMACS post-processing workflow to extract and validate the temperature time series of pure water at 300 K from an energy file.",
  "required_inputs": [
    "nvt.edr",
    "npt.edr",
    "md.edr",
    "topol.top"
  ],
  "expected_gromacs_commands": [
    "echo Temperature | gmx energy -f nvt.edr -o temperature_nvt.xvg",
    "echo Temperature | gmx energy -f npt.edr -o temperature_npt.xvg",
    "echo Temperature | gmx energy -f md.edr -o temperature_md.xvg"
  ],
  "important_rules": [
    "Use non-interactive echo-based gmx energy selection.",
    "Explain which energy file belongs to NVT, NPT, and production MD.",
    "Explain that temperature should fluctuate around 300 K after equilibration.",
    "Do not treat a very short trajectory as final proof of equilibration.",
    "Mention that temperature stability alone is not sufficient; density, energy, pressure, and volume should also be checked for full equilibration validation."
  ],
  "expected_outputs": [
    "temperature_nvt.xvg",
    "temperature_npt.xvg",
    "temperature_md.xvg"
  ],
  "common_errors": [
    "Using the wrong .edr file.",
    "Not explaining the interactive energy selection.",
    "Assuming temperature stability alone proves full equilibration.",
    "Reporting a short trajectory as final convergence evidence."
  ],
  "validation_note": "The workflow is valid if temperature time-series files are extracted and show fluctuations around 300 K after equilibration. Temperature alone is not sufficient for full equilibration validation.",
  "_source_file": "gscf_010_water_temperature_analysis_300K.json"
}
